title: Missouri Revised Statutes § 233.103 - No limit on amounts spent within cities (Clay and Platte counties)
text:
233.103.  No limit on amounts spent within cities (Clay and Platte counties). — 1.  The limitations on amounts which may be expended upon roads and streets within the corporate limits of any city, as provided in sections 233.095 and 233.100, shall be inapplicable in a county of the first class not having a charter form of government or of the second class which contains all or part of a city having a population of three hundred fifty thousand or more.
  2.  In such a county, the revenue set aside and credited to a road district may, with the consent of a city, town or village located within the district, be expended within such incorporated city, town or village.
(L. 1965 p. 369 § 1, A.L. 1974 H.B. 1733)
